title: Food business marketing website (React + TS + Tailwind + shadcn/ui)

requirements.summary: Build a responsive multi-page marketing site with Home, Menu, About/Story, and Contact pages and clear client-side navigation (English text only).
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation allows switching between Home, Menu, About/Story, and Contact without page reload issues.
Menu page displays at least 3 categories and at least 3 items per category, each with name, short description, and price.
Contact page includes visible contact details (at minimum: phone and/or email and/or address placeholders) and a working contact form UI with validation messages in English.
Layout is mobile-friendly and remains readable and usable on small screens.

file_operations.description: Create the app shell with client-side routing for Home, Menu, About/Story, and Contact, plus shared layout (header/footer) and navigation links. Use the shadcn-ui components (e.g., Button, Separator) for consistent navigation styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a reusable layout component that renders a consistent header (nav) and footer across all pages, including responsive behavior (mobile menu or stacked nav). Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Button, Sheet/Dropdown if available) to compose the header/nav. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement the Home page with a hero section and business highlights (English copy), structured to be responsive and readable on small screens. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card, Badge) for highlight sections. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement the Menu page with at least 3 categories and at least 3 items per category (name, short description, price), rendered with a responsive grid/stacked layout. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card) for menu item presentation. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement the About/Story page with the business story and values (English copy), with a responsive section layout and consistent typography. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card) as needed. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement the Contact page structure with visible contact details (phone/email/address placeholders in English) and include the contact form section (wired in REQ-3). Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card) for page sections. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add static menu data (at least 3 categories, 3 items each) to power the Menu page rendering (name/description/price).

requirements.summary: Apply a cohesive warm/earthy visual theme (no blue/purple primary palette) with consistent typography, spacing, and component styling across pages.
acceptanceCriteria: All pages share consistent header/footer styling, typography, and button/input styles.
Primary palette is not blue/purple; theme is clearly food/restaurant appropriate (e.g., warm neutrals, earthy tones, etc.).
The site has a polished, cohesive look with consistent spacing and section structure.

file_operations.description: Create global styles and Tailwind layer setup, and define a warm/earthy theme via CSS variables used by shadcn/ui tokens (avoid blue/purple as primary). Ensure consistent base typography, spacing defaults, and background/foreground colors.
Adjust Tailwind theme extensions as needed to support the chosen warm/earthy palette and typography (e.g., font families), keeping compatibility with existing CSS variable-based color tokens.

Refine header/footer styling to match the global theme (spacing, borders, background, typographic hierarchy) and ensure consistent button/link styling. Use shadcn-ui components to keep styling consistent. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Implement contact form submission via the Motoko backend capability and display success/error feedback with basic client-side validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Contact form collects at least: name, email (or phone), and message.
Submitting the form stores the submission in the backend (in canister memory).
UI shows a success state after a successful submission and an error state if the backend call fails.
Basic client-side validation prevents empty required fields from being submitted.

file_operations.description: Create a small typed frontend wrapper that uses the core-infrastructure backend connection configuration to call the backend capability submitContactForm(name, email, message). (No backend changes here.)

Build the contact form UI (name, email, message) with client-side validation and English validation messages. Use React Query for submission state (loading/success/error). Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Form primitives if present, Input, Textarea, Button, Alert) for consistent styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire the ContactForm component into the Contact page and render success/error feedback states clearly after submission attempts. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Alert) for feedback styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add required generated static images under frontend/public/assets/generated and use them in the UI (hero banner and logo mark) without serving images from the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated images exist at frontend/public/assets/generated and are referenced directly by the frontend.
Home page uses the generated hero image in the hero section.
Site header/footer (or home hero) displays the generated logo image.

file_operations.description: Add the generated static hero banner image asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-food.dim_1600x900.png and ensure it is referenced directly by the frontend.
Add the generated static logo mark asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/logo-mark.dim_512x512.png and ensure it is referenced directly by the frontend.

Reference frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-food.dim_1600x900.png in the Home hero section and incorporate frontend/public/assets/generated/logo-mark.dim_512x512.png in the hero/branding area. Ensure responsive image presentation.
Display frontend/public/assets/generated/logo-mark.dim_512x512.png in the site header (and/or footer) alongside the business name for consistent branding across pages.
